title: HeadObject, GetObject, Conditions, Ranges, and Cleanup · Go Semantics Case: Head Vs Get
summary: A download service calls GET to discover metadata, leaks response bodies, retries after sending headers, and returns full private objects for malformed ranges. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the head vs get increment in bounded S3 download decision and cleanup flow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose HEAD, GET, conditional GET, or a metadata database lookup without doubling requests or trusting stale authority.
Finalize by matching intent to HEAD or event evidence, checksum, version, size, owner, scan result, and idempotent state transition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Head Vs Get · Theory Head Vs Get
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 4721 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned conditional range and prove S3 response-body cleanup; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
